body.title: Add structured call-log to BaseShim for inter-agent observability
body.result: 18 tests proving dispatch() JSONL trace mechanism (success/raises/unknown-tool paths); record uses device_id; call-path migration to dispatch() is future work
body.description:
Inter-agent tool calls currently go through the shim but are not logged in a structured way. Add a call-log to BaseShim.dispatch() (or equivalent dispatch entry point): device_id, tool_name, latency_ms, success, error_type. Written to datacenter_logs/shim/trace/<date>.jsonl. This gives inspectability of all inter-agent traffic without requiring agents to change — the shim is the natural interception point.

**Affected files:** unseen_university/skeleton/base_shim.py (or equivalent), datacenter_logs/shim/trace/ (created at runtime)
**Design rules:** log hierarchy is datacenter_logs/<device>/<subsystem>/; no new DB tables
**Scope boundary:** IN — structured JSONL log per tool dispatch through any BaseShim subclass; OUT — real-time stream, alerting, metrics aggregation
**Completion criteria:** after any MCP tool call through a shim subclass, a JSONL record appears in datacenter_logs/shim/trace/ with device, tool_name, latency_ms, success fields
